Football.title: Michael Carrick’s secret weapon on coaching staff is Man Utd’s eye in the sky and has special bond with Kobbie Mainoo
Football.content:
HE may not be a household name, but Travis Binnion is famous in Michael Carrick’s world.

When questioned about his high-profile backroom staff, the United head coach bluntly makes a point of adding “and Trav…” into his answers.





But Binnion, who stepped up from the Under-21s after Ruben Amorim was sacked in January, has become popular with the ones that count – the players.

Sources inside Old Trafford are struck by how quickly the 39-year-old settled into a tough position of authority with the senior squad.

Binnion is particularly close to Harry Maguire, who was one of the first players to welcome him into the fold in January.

Binnion’s special bond with 20-year-old Kobbie Mainoo is also noted, as he helped coach the midfielder since his U14 days.

One insider revealed everybody at the club, including Binnion, knew he was walking into a trial by fire with the first team, who would waste no time deciding if he was up to the job.

Before Carrick’s arrival, Binnion had already been promoted from the Under-21s to support Darren Fletcher‘s brief two-game caretaker run.

Both Carrick and director of football Jason Wilcox decided he should stay to help bridge the gap between the first team and the academy.

United sources revealed Binnion plays the biggest role in choosing which youth players train with the first team weekly.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

It’s understood his unique knowledge of players coming through and close relationship with Fletcher helps support the club’s long-term development.

Well-placed sources said it is a massive step up from the Amorim era, when the Portuguese requested youngsters as temporary cover for specific positions or drills.

Just last week Binnion advised Carrick that U18 defender Godwill Kukonki was ready to join the Premier League matchday squad at St James’ Park.



Likewise, last month Binnion pushed for Jack Moorhouse to be included at Everton to make up the numbers for Lisandro Martinez’s hamstring injury.

Binnion has been supporting Moorhouse since his return from loan at Leyton Orient in January and advised other players on temporary moves.

Positioned in the press box with five analysts, Binnion acts as the eye in the sky.

SunSport can exclusively reveal that he feeds real-time directions through an earpiece to Jonathan Woodgate on the touchline.

During the Newcastle defeat, Binnion and the analysts missed Casemiro’s equaliser at the end of a hectic first-half stoppage time.



Dressed in United tracksuits, the group faced a barrage of abuse from irate home fans – incensed by Jacob Ramsey’s red card – as they made their way to the dressing room at half-time.

Binnion plays a major role in pre-match warm-ups and teams up with Jonny Evans to run post-game cool downs for subs.

Sources said the entire coaching staff works collectively on set-pieces, but Evans takes responsibility for the bulk of the deadball analysis.

Binnion has also been working closely with younger signings such as Benjamin Sesko and Patrick Dorgu.

At West Ham at the start of February, Binnion was the last one in from the warm-ups with his arm around Joshua Zirkzee, who is struggling for game-time.



Sesko told journalists after his match-winner against Everton that he’s grateful for the extra work Binnion has put into his one-on-one sessions after training.

Despite Binnion’s close work with forwards, he was a defender many moons ago.

Born in Derby, Binnion came through the ranks at Sheffield United before a brutal injury robbed him of a playing career.

Ahead of the FA Youth Cup final in 2022, he told PA: “I was 18, just coming up to my 19th birthday, playing a game and just went to kick a ball.



“The midfielder I was playing against nicked it and I just felt a pain in my hip. I didn’t kick a ball again for 18 months.”

Binnion went under the knife three times before returning to action, signing with Finnish side IFK Mariehamn in April 2008 before the problem reappeared.

He added: “It went again so I found myself out of contract and in need of both hips operating on.

“I got a lot of help – help off the surgeon, help off my parents – but I just got to the point where it wasn’t right.

“I could function in day-to-day life but I found myself unemployed and in debt.”



Binnion was hired by John Pemberton to help coach development sides at Bramall Lane in 2009 – months after Carrick won the Champions League with Sir Alex Ferguson.

Insiders at United say Binnion’s wealth of life experience makes him the perfect mentor to help young players weather adversity, particularly those who face the heartbreak of being released.

He said in 2023: “I want people to have the career that I didn’t have.

“My pitfalls, even without the injuries, and maybe how I didn’t develop my game to the optimum.

“Just things you learn along the way, good and bad from your coaches in formative years.



“You sort of collect all that and I know I’ve always had a good work ethic.

“I’ve been brought up the right way, so I always want to work hard and improve and put those qualities into the players in my care and the staff in my care.”

Binnion was actually in the opposing dugout for the 2011 Youth Cup final, assisting a Blades side containing Maguire against a victorious United squad boasting Paul Pogba and Jesse Lingard.



He quickly worked his way up the ranks to become Sheffield United’s academy manager when Nick Cox left for United in 2016.

Cox then brought him to Carrington in 2019 to head player development for the U14s and U16s, before he became U18s lead coach in 2021 – when Carrick was working under Ole Gunnar Solskjaer.

There is a feeling at United that Binnion – who may not be a glamorous ideological tactician – is one of the best coaches to arrive at the club in the last decade.

During a dismal 2022 club campaign, Binnion provided a rare highlight by leading Mainoo and Alejandro Garnacho’s side to a record-extending 11th Youth Cup title.

He played a key role in developing players like Shea Lacey, who transitioned into first-team training under his guidance.



Binnion feels he has benefited from watching Amorim’s training sessions when the teens were called up.

At Carrington academy games, you’ll typically find Carrick alongside his assistants Steve Holland and Woodgate.

Binnion, meanwhile, sits amongst the U18 coaches, taking the time to catch up with colleagues and the medical team.

He boasts a unique sporting background, having made a single professional appearance in a 2002 List A cricket match for Nottinghamshire.

Binnion will stay on with Carrick if he gets the job long-term this summer. But his U21 role awaits him if a big name arrives at Old Trafford.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Adam Lawrence was called back in to manage the group for the final part of the season after resigning from Newcastle.

Plenty at Carrington believe that Binnion will realise his dream of being a manager one day.

Football.title: Chelsea player ratings: Hato and Garnacho impress but two players get just 2/10 in FA Cup win over Wrexham
Football.content:
HOLLYWOOD. That’s a fitting word to describe this game, as Chelsea scraped their way past Ryan Reynolds’ Wrexham into the FA Cup quarter-final.

It was a fast and furious start from the spirited hosts, who took the lead after 17 minutes as Sam Smith buried the ball past Robert Sanchez with aplomb.





Before half-time though, Chelsea found a way back into the game through some calamitous Wrexham defending – a goal-line clearance rebounded off former Arsenal goalie Arthur Okwonko and into the back of the net.

Both sides rang in the changes in the second half, with Marc Cucurella returning from injury and Dario Essugo making his first competitive Chelsea appearance.

It was Wrexham sub Josh Windass that made the difference, though striking a deflected volley into the back of the Chelsea net to send Wrexham fans wild, only for the game to be drawn level minutes later by Josh Acheampong.

George Dobson was sent off for a horror tackle on Alejandro Garnacho, before being given a STANDING OVATION by the home supporters for the act.



Garnacho though, had the last laugh, scoring the winner on the volley in extra-time.

There was some shine added to the scoreline as Joao Pedro came off the bench to add a fourth.

SunSport’s Chelsea reporter Lloyd Canfield has given his ratings from the Racecourse Ground…

ROBERT SANCHEZ – 4

Chelsea fans chant about Robert Sanchez goes: “He used to be s**** now he’s alright, walking in a Sanchez wonderland”.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

This seemed more like last season’s Sanchez than the solid keeper we’ve seen for large parts of this campaign …

Should’ve done better for the Wrexham opener, though admittedly did save the Blues on a few occasions where they could’ve found themselves conceding more.

JOSH ACHEAMPONG – 6

Struggled at wing-back for Chelsea, and would’ve been better served playing at centre-half.

Played very high up the pitch which doesn’t make the most of his best qualities, which are defensive, though he did improve somewhat in the second half.



Bagged a vital Chelsea equaliser with a good finish into the roof of the net to keep the Blues in the game.

TOSIN ADARABIOYO – 2

Poor.

Even with two defenders either side of him he didn’t look good enough, and was left wanting for the Wrexham opener.

It’s tough really to praise anything about his performance in this one, as it was when he was dropped by Enzo Maresca earlier in the season for a similar performance against Leeds.



MAMADOU SARR – 5

Was probably the best of the three Chelsea central defenders, before confusingly being replaced by Marc Guiu after 57 minutes.

Was decent in the ball and fairly strong in his tackles, particularly impressive considering he is fasting due to Ramadan, and broke his fast in the first half.

BENOIT BADIASHILE – 2

Too slow. This is supposed to be a Champions League level centre-back who would look more at home in the Championship.

JORREL HATO – 7

Definitely the best of the Chelsea defenders on the pitch, and one of few players that could hold their head high after this game.



Has plenty of room for improvement, but today was a good performance, showing he can invert well, defend well 1v1, and pass nicely going forwards too.

Came close to giving Chelsea the lead in the second half before being replaced by the returning Marc Cucurella.

ANDREY SANTOS – 3

Ghosted in midfield in a match he should’ve dominated.



Too slow on the ball and too slow off it, Santos needed to demand the ball more and make things happen with his passing, which he struggled to do in this game.

Has had bright moments this season, and today should’ve been a match to build on that, but he didn’t.

ROMEO LAVIA – 3

Has been good for the Blues as he continues his return from a lengthy period of suffering with injuries.

Decent at recycling the ball and moving it on in midfield, but did look a little off the pace, getting an early booking after being beaten with ease in midfield.

The first time since coming back that he has looked like a player returning to fitness…



PEDRO NETO – 3

After a hat-trick against Hull City in the last round, fans were half expecting him to slice through the Wrexham defence like Deadpool himself.

He didn’t.

Showed a few half-decent flashes on the ball, but was far from the Maverick we saw against Hull, and didn’t seem up to scratch in this one.

Hit the bar late on

ALEJANDRO GARNACHO – 7

It was Garnacho’s strike that forced Chelsea’s first half leveller, but giving him the credit would feel generous as the shot wasn’t great…



Ran like Turbo down the left wing, and eventually got his reward for plenty of hard work when he volleyed home in extra-time to secure the win for the Blues.

Chelsea’s most dangerous player on the night by a distance.

LIAM DELAP – 6

Up against three Wrexham defenders, his physicality was useful and vital in the Chelsea equaliser, for which he did really well to roll his man and feed Garnacho.



It was a selfless showing from the Blues forward who is desperate for goals, but ultimately he worked hard to do his bit for the team.

SUBS:

MARC GUIU (MAMADOU SARR, 58) – 4

Didn’t have the impact he would’ve hoped for from the bench, having not played for a long time.

Spurned a headed chance wide of the post late on.

MARC CUCURELLA (JORREL HATO, 65) – 5

Returned from injury in this game, and the minutes in his tank will be needed.

He wasn’t on the ball an awful lot, but got forward nicely and showed flashes of what he can do.



DARIO ESSUGO (ROMEO LAVIA, 65) – 6

Made his first competitive Chelsea appearance, replacing Romeo Lavia.

In fairness to him, he didn’t look at all rusty and settled into a tense game nicely with some key blocks and tackles.

Secured the assist for Garnacho’s winner.

JOAO PEDRO (LIAM DELAP, 84) – 5

Chelsea’s man of the moment would’ve hoped to be rested in this game, but had no such luck, he was needed.

Injected a bit of life into the Blues frontline with a few good passes into dangerous positions.



He was rewarded for his impact with a goal to seal Chelsea’s passage into the next round.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

MALO GUSTO (JOSH ACHEAMPONG, 84)

Made some important tackles and blocks after coming on

JESSE DERRY (PEDRO NETO, ET) – 5

Almost immediately hacked down after coming on in extra time, but wasn’t afraid to show some neat touches on the ball and do his due diligence defensively.

Football.title: Lionel Messi’s staggering wages revealed by Inter Miami owner – but how does pay compare to Cristiano Ronaldo’s salary?
Football.content:
THE SALARIES earned by Lionel Messi at Inter Miami and Cristiano Ronaldo at Al-Nassr are incredible, but there’s one clear winner.

Inter Miami co-owner Jorge Mas admitted this week that the Argentine icon earns around $70million (£52m) to $80m (£60m) a year in total.





Mas insisted the MLS club are happy to foot the enormous bill for the 38-year-old superstar.

As revealed by The Athletic, Mas said: “The reason that I need to have sponsors and for them to be world class is because players are expensive.”

“I pay Messi, worth every penny, but it’s $70m to $80m a year. Across everything.”

According to the MLS Players’ Association, Messi earns a $12m base salary with $20.45m in guaranteed compensation, which includes bonuses.

But his contract also includes a future ownership stake in Inter Miami, dramatically boosting the total value of his deal.

The Argentine legend signed a new three-year extension in October, committing his future to the Florida club until 2028.

Since arriving in the United States, Messi has transformed the club.

He inspired Miami to the 2025 MLS title, scoring 29 goals and claiming the Golden Boot in a sensational campaign.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Messi also sits comfortably as the highest-paid player in MLS.

But even Messi’s massive payday pales in comparison to the fortune being earned by Ronaldo in Saudi Arabia.

The Portuguese megastar signed a new two-year contract with Al-Nassr last June, extending his stay in Riyadh until 2027.

It was revealed when he signed his deal, that it is worth nearly £178m per year, which is around TRIPLE Messi’s annual earnings.

The contract is widely regarded as the most lucrative in sporting history, with Ronaldo reportedly earning £488,000 per day in basic wages.



His total package could exceed £492m over two years once bonuses, sponsorships and perks are included.

The financial comparison is just the latest chapter in one of sport’s greatest ever rivalries.

Messi and Ronaldo, now 38 and 41, have dominated football for almost two decades – claiming 13 Ballon d’Or titles between them.



Messi leads the race with eight, while Ronaldo has five. The pair have finished first and second NINE times in the award’s history.

Even as they approach the latter stages of their careers, both remain key figures for their countries.

Ronaldo continues to lead Portugal, and Messi remains Argentina’s talisman.

And with another FIFA World Cup looming this summer, the two icons are still performing on the global stage.



Despite entering his 40s, Ronaldo remains prolific in Saudi Arabia.

Since joining Al-Nassr in the 2022/23 season, he has scored 121 goals in 137 appearances for the club.

Meanwhile, Messi continues to dazzle fans in MLS while boosting the league’s global profile.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The debate over which player is the true GOAT has raged for years.



But when it comes to wages right now, Ronaldo’s Saudi fortune comfortably leaves Messi trailing behind.

Football.title: Wrexham 2 Chelsea 4: Garnacho and Pedro break Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney’s hearts with extra-time FA Cup goals
Football.content:
AT LEAST they will get a good episode out of it.

For so long Wrexham and their tinsel-town ownership looked to have delivered the dream storyline for their Disney documentary.





Twice they led against Chelsea, so often they made the World Champions suffer and look average. But not every story has a happy ending.

There were so many candidates for Phil Parkinson’s leading man. Sam Smith bagged the opener, set up by Callum Doyle who scored their second after a Arthur Okonkwo own-goal.

But the Blues provided the villains. Josh Acheampong forcing extra-time before Alejandro Garnacho’s winner.

It was all watched, of course, by Wrexham’s Hollywood owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney, joined by the former’s wife Blake Lively.



They stood in the directors’ box with a boom microphone hovering above their heads and as many cameras trained on them as the pitch.

An ownership duo to make Chelsea’s big-spending American overlords look stuffy and reserved.

They should only take encouragement from this. Offering a true test to a much changed but still high-level side.

For a team who, three years ago to the day, were facing Dagenham & Redbridge in the National League this was always going to be a day to celebrate and further whet the appetite.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

With perhaps more than one eye on Paris Saint-Germain next week, Rosenior made nine changes and also bizarrely matched Wrexham’s back three.

For a while it looked like Rosenior had been too clever for his own good.

Parkinson raised some eyebrows, benching his two top scorers in Kieffer Moore and Josh Windass, though they had their own big midweek game, against promotion rivals Hull, to consider.

Smith was the one entrusted with leading the line, given what looked to be a lonely task – but one he embraced with relish to more than make his mark.



Despite having three central defenders up against him, not one of them kept pace when he raced on to Doyle’s 70-yard thump from the back and gave the Disney cameras dotted around the Racecourse their dream moment.

The centre-half signed from Manchester City feeding the striker snapped up from Reading, another reminder of the ambition and aptitude of those in charge in North Wales.

A moment to savour, though one that left Rosenior in a rage.



His defenders near gave up the chase and Robert Sanchez, who had been dropped as first choice keeper in midweek but restored here, did not cover himself in glory – hesitating on the edge of his box before retreating and allowing Smith the space to roll his one-on-one in and spark bedlam and shake the Blues.

Sanchez was soon saving from Max Cleworth and Ryan Longman, those efforts either side of Smith passing up another huge chance, trying to cut inside but ending up on his backside.

Chelsea’s reply, for some time, was non-existent. Liam Delap was battling to make things happen to little avail as the hosts held firm.



Just before the break, however, the striker finally forced his way through with some old fashioned brute force.

Sanchez, seeking redemption, pinged a low and very risky ball to Delap’s feet on the halfway line.

The bulky No9 shoved and turned Dom Hyam in impressive fashion before charging forward and picking out Alejandro Garnacho on the left.



The Argentine let fly and watched George Thomason clear off the line but into the back of his goalkeeper, Okonkwo and in.

Chelsea hardly deserved it but they were level – an admirable response to a test of their ever-questioned mental fortitude.

Chesea upped the tempo after the break but Wrexham were not put off.



Still battling, the Racecourse still full-throated in its support and the World Champions still uncomfortable.

Chelsea were trying to make their class count, Jorrel Hato pulling off a stunning spin but firing wide, but the hosts continued to create and cause trouble.

Their pursuit of a historic upset was bolstered by Windass and Moore’s introduction – which soon bore fruit.

Windass struck as a clearance from a succession of corners fell to him, his low effort incredibly flicked in by defender Doyle. McElhenney stood, hands on head as supporters danced with replica Cups in the stands.



Their celebrations were quickly doused, with Acheampong responding within minutes – firing home a bobbling ball from Andrey Santos before chaos ensued.

Thomson stung Sanchez’s gloves with a volley, Pedro Neto clattered the bar at the other end before George Dobson sent Garnacho flying with a brute of a challenge – earning a red card after a review which really should not have been required.

Wrexham held off to force extra time, but fell to the winner soon after.

Garnacho was jeered from start to finish. Six minutes into extra-time he responded, volleying home Dario Essugo’s cross – though there was confusion, and hope, after the ball ricocheted off the base of the net and out.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

It was a cruel twist for those who thought it had been kept out, and another followed when Lewis Brunt saw a leveller ruled out for offside five minutes from time.

Chelsea spent most of the final minutes looking content to shoot themselves in the foot until, finally, Joao Pedro rolled one in and declared, “The End.”

Football.title: Bukayo Saka posts brilliant four-word response after Mansfield fan holds up offensive shirt about Arsenal star
Football.content:
BUKAYO SAKA wrote “love you guys too” after sharing a picture of an Arsenal shirt featuring his name and an expletive.

The England star, 24, came off the bench as the Gunners won 2-1 at Mansfield in the FA Cup.





There was plenty of FA Cup excitement at Field Mill, with owners John and Carolyn Radford even telling SunSport this week that two of their children are Arsenal supporters.

While the Radfords were happy to wear half-and-half scarves, not all locals gave Saka and Co such a warm welcome.

After the match, Arsenal’s No7 shared a picture of a fan raising an Arsenal shirt with “Saka sh**” printed on the back.

The winger posted the snap alongside the message “love you guys too” and a laughing emoji.



Arsenal were given a scare by their League One opponents.

Mikel Arteta‘s side took the lead via Noni Madueke on the stroke of half time.

But Will Evans levelled for Nigel Clough’s men just five minutes into the second period.

Eberechi Eze‘s 66th minute effort turned out to be enough to send Arsenal through.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

But Arteta saw enough threat in the home side to still throw talisman Saka on for Max Dowman after 77 minutes.

Asked about the tough challenge his side were made to navigate, Arteta was complimentary of Mansfield after the match.

The Gunners boss said: “Yes, and we knew that, but that’s the beauty of this competition.

“There’s the magic, the context, the opposition, the pitch, the crowd, which I think they made it beautiful because they put so much energy, intensity, a bit of banter as well at times, and that’s the beauty of this competition.



“That’s why it is unique, that’s why everybody loves playing it, and you have to earn it, and it took a while to make sure that we had a game.”

Arsenal will be back in action on Wednesday when they face Bayer Leverkusen in Germany in the first leg of their Champions League round of 16 clash.

Football.title: Real Madrid secretly scout Newcastle star during FA Cup clash vs Man City ahead of huge summer battle
Football.content:
REAL MADRID have joined Arsenal in the race for Sandro Tonali.

Scouts from the LaLiga giants are watching Toon’s FA Cup clash against Manchester City on Saturday night.





Madrid spies are there to plot to topple Pep Guardiola’s side ahead of their upcoming Champions League last-16 showdown against the Cityzens.

But SunSport understands Madrid staff have actually embarked on a double mission and with the 25-year-old Italian midfielder also high on their agenda.

And they are preparing to join City and Arsenal as Toon brace themselves for an expected £100m transfer tussle this summer over their midfield magician.

Newcastle sold Alexander Isak for a British record £130m to Liverpool last year and are determined not to lose another star.



Suggestions that Tonali has indicated he wants to stay next season are so far wide of the mark, though SunSport understands there has been no communication yet he wants to leave either.

His wife Juliette gave birth to baby boy Leonardo in January and his family are happy in the North East and are not actively looking for an exit.

The player’s agent Giuseppe Riso attempted to play down talk that his client was trying to engineer a move after deadline day links with Arsenal too.

However, Riso then muddied the waters by stating Tonali’s true value would be known in March – which indicated his asking price could increase if he helps Italy qualify for the World Cup via the play-offs.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Newcastle will fight to keep Tonali, who is on a £150,000-a-week contract effectively until 2030, at St James’ Park but the club are aware awkward discussions could follow if they miss out on qualifying for European football.

And the potential lure of becoming a Galactico at the Bernabeu could be a hard one to resist for the ex-AC Milan ace.

It also seems unlikely that should a whopping offer arrive on the table and Tonali indicates an interest in pursuing it then they would embark on another summer-long saga like they did with striker Isak.

Tonali does enjoy a close relationship with Eddie Howe and remains grateful for the club’s backing during his ten-month betting ban shortly after his £55m arrival in 2023 from Milan.



But when asked directly by SunSport back in November about his long-term future, Tonali spoke honestly about the uncertainty of a footballer’s life.

He said: “This is a tough question because, you know [in] football you need to think year for year. The last summer was tough for us, for Alex [Isak], but this is football.

“If you have an option for your life, for another team you need to think about everything. I don’t want to say, ‘Yes, I want to stay here ten years,’ but now I’m happy here. I don’t think anything about another team.”

Football.title: ‘Don’t think they realised what was going on’ – Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney confused during Wrexham vs Chelsea
Football.content:
RYAN REYNOLDS and Rob McElhenney were teased following their reactions to Wrexham’s opener against Chelsea.

The Hollywood duo were both in attendance as their beloved Championship side hosted the Blues in the FA Cup.





Forward Sam Smith, 24, fired Wrexham into the lead on 18 minutes against the two-time European champions.

The goal sparked pandemonium… but was subject to a VAR check.

From the fifth round onwards, VAR is implemented across all FA Cup matches – even those at grounds outside of the Premier League.

So fans – including co-owners Reynolds and McElhenney – were subjected to the sort of nervous wait that they do not have to contend with during league matches.



Fans chanted expletives about the tech as the check went on.

But BBC commentator Guy Mowbray, calling the game alongside Alan Shearer, didn’t think the A-listers quite grasped that their side’s goal had been hanging in the balance.

A further roar spread across the Racecourse Ground when the goal was confirmed.

And Mowbray cheekily said: “Don’t think Ryan and Rob had quite realised what was going on there for a moment.”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Reynolds, 49, even appeared to be on a video call when TV cameras panned to him immediately after Smith’s strike.

Reynolds was joined in the directors’ box by fellow star wife Blake Lively.

Wrexham’s lead lasted more than 20 minutes before they were pegged back in unfortunate circumstances.



Alejandro Garnacho’s goal-bound shot was clawed off the line by George Thomason.

But Thomason’s clearance struck floored goalkeeper Arthur Okonkwo and trickled into the net for 1-1.

Wrexham reclaimed the lead on 79 minutes via Callum Doyle’s clever flick.

But Chelsea quickly levelled via Josh Acheampong.

There would be more VAR fury among the locals in stoppage time, with referee Peter Bankes sending off George Dobson for a fierce challenge on Garnacho after consulting the pitchside monitor.

Football.title: EFL clash descends into chaos as players including ex-Premier League star and manager are forced apart after ugly brawl
Football.content:
SWINDON’S clash against Crawley descended into chaos as a former Premier League star and manager were forced apart.

The Robins travelled to West Sussex to take on the Red Devils in League Two as they looked to close the gap on the automatic play-off spots.





Crawley would have been hoping for a positive result to boost their survival hopes.

However, the game was overshadowed as an ugly brawl broke out between the two sides.

Tempers flared after full-time, which saw multiple players and staff from both sides clash.

Swindon’s Junior Hoilett could be seen in the brawl trying to diffuse the situation.

The former Premier League, 35, star with Blackburn, QPR and Cardiff City was spotted trying to pull goalkeeper Connor Ripley away from the bust-up.

The stopper was clashing with the Crawley staff in the middle of the brawl.

Manager Scott Lindsey was also involved in the clash as he was reeling with the decision to award Swindon a penalty.

Speaking to media afterwards, he said: “It’s the worst decision I’ve ever seen in football and that’s a fact.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“I don’t know how many minutes later Jonny Russell goes through and Will Wright brings him down with a clear penalty and we don’t get it.

“There was an elbow to Kellan Gordon off the side, Gordon gets crashed into the side barriers, nothing’s done.

“I thought first half he actually refereed it alright and then he just, I don’t know what to say. It’s just incredible what I’ve just seen, just like incredible.”

The clash sparked after the full-time whistle as Crawley snatched a last-minute equaliser.

Klaidi Lolos had given the hosts the lead before the Robins hit back with goals from Aaron Drinan and Billy Bodin.



Bodin’s goal came in the 91st minute, leading to the visitors thinking they had snatched all three points.

But the leveller came even later as Kellan Gordon found the net in the 97th minute to split the spoils.

The point helps Crawley’s plight and moves them one point ahead of Newport in the relegation area.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Swindon stay fifth in the table but could see Notts County, Cambridge Town and MK Dons all pull away if they win their games in hand.



Hoilett joined the Robins in February on a contract until the end of the season after leaving Scottish outfit Hibernian.

Football.title: Wrexham get mixed VAR verdict as Wayne Rooney and Alan Shearer disagree on Chelsea decision
Football.content:
Wrexham get mixed VAR verdict as Wayne Rooney and Alan Shearer disagree on Chelsea decision

George Dobson was sent off for a poor challenge on Alejandro Garnacho following a VAR review and the pundits were split over the decision by referee Peter Bankes

View 2 Images George Dobson was sent off after a VAR review (Image: James Gill/Getty Images)

Wayne Rooney and Alan Shearer had differing opinions on George Dobson's red card in Wrexham's defeat by Chelsea in the FA Cup. Dobson was dismissed after a VAR review in the 93rd minute and Chelsea made their numerical advantage count by scoring twice in extra time to win 4-2.

‌



The Wrexham midfielder was initially shown a yellow card after bringing down Alejandro Garnacho to prevent a Chelsea break late in normal time. But referee Peter Bankes was sent over to the pitchside monitor by VAR Paul Howard and upgraded the card to a red.

‌



Dobson caught the Chelsea winger on the shin and his challenge was deemed to be forceful and therefore endangering his opponent. There was much debate about the call on the BBC's coverage.

‌



“It is the right decision,” ex-Wrexham defender Ben Tozer said. “Listen, he has to make the foul because he is thinking I need to delay the play and take a sensible yellow card. Unfortunately, he catches (him) and Wayne actually thought it was a good tackle, which I found ironic. He has to foul him but it is a red card. His studs have gone into the side of his shin. For me, it is a red card.”

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Chelsea break Wrexham hearts after VAR drama in instant FA Cup classic - 6 talking points READ MORE :

However, asked if he felt a yellow card would have been the correct call, Rooney said: “Yeah, I think so. It is a little bit high and that is about it. It is not a straight leg and full studs going into him. I’m not too sure.”

‌



Tozer then said: “It is like a dark orange, isn’t it? It is one of them, if it happens on you, you are absolutely fuming.” Rooney concluded: “I’m still not sure. I don’t think it is red.”

Reacting to the decision on commentary, Alan Shearer said: “It is hard to argue against it. You cannot have any complaints.”

View 2 Images George Dobson saw his yellow card upgraded to red (Image: TNT Sports)

‌



HAVE YOUR SAY! What did you make of the decision? Comment below.

That mixed verdict continued post-match when both managers were asked for their opinion. "It's our first taste of VAR, but I thought it had to be clear and obvious for the decision to be changed," Wrexham's Phil Parkinson told TNT Sports. "Dobbo has gone in with force, the ref is in a good position to see it, he's scraped his skin and caught him lower down. I think he could easily have just kept it as a yellow and, in my opinion, I don't think the VAR should have intervened in that.

"I think anyone who watches it will think it's a tight call and if it's a tight call you should stick with the original decision. So, yeah, a first taste at the Racecourse Ground of VAR and obviously it hurts that it's such an influence tonight."

‌



Liam Rosenior felt the right decision was reached. "It's dangerous," he said. "I don't ever want to see a player sent off but, for me, Ale could have been really badly injured from that challenge so they're the challenges that you don't want to see."

Football.title: Chelsea break Wrexham hearts after VAR drama in instant FA Cup classic - 6 talking points
Football.content:
Chelsea break Wrexham hearts after VAR drama in instant FA Cup classic - 6 talking points

WREXHAM 2-4 CHELSEA: A much-changed Blues side just get the job done in extra time - with thanks to VAR - after being pushed hard by their Championship opponents

View 6 Images Alejandro Garnacho scored in extra time for Chelsea (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

Alejandro Garnacho struck in extra time as Chelsea twice came from behind to see off a spirited Wrexham side and reach the quarter finals of the FA Cup. Wrexham more than matched their Premier League opponents, twice led and had a last-gasp equaliser chalked off by VAR, but a red card and Chelsea's strength off the bench made the difference in north Wales.

‌



The home side took the lead when Sam Smith latched onto a long ball, beat the offside trap, sprinted clear of the Chelsea defence and placed a finish past Robert Sanchez. The Blues goalkeeper was forced into two more saves before they managed to equalise due to a slice of luck.

‌



Garnacho's effort was cleared off the line by George Thomason, only for the ball to cannon off the back of goalkeeper Arthur Okonkwo and go in. Liam Rosenior rang the changes in the second half, but initially without the desired effect, and Wrexham deservedly took the lead once more.

‌



Chelsea failed to clear a corner and Josh Windass' powerful low drive was inventively flicked in by Callum Doyle to make it 2-1. The Premier League side responded immediately, with Josh Acheampong smashing into the roof of the net from a tight angle.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Pedro Neto smashed the bar from a cutback before George Dobson was sent off, following a VAR review, for taking out Garnacho with a poor challenge to stop a break. Chelsea's quality and man advantage told in extra time as Garnacho volleyed Dario Essugo's cross in at the back post to go 3-2 up.

‌



There was still time for more drama as Lewis Brunt thought he'd made it 3-3 from a corner. But VAR replays showed he was offside when Kieffer Moore flicked the ball on at the near post, breaking Wrexham hearts.

Lewis O'Brien curled another great chance wide before substitute Joao Pedro settled it with a piece of quality, finding the bottom corner in the dying moments. Here are the talking points from an FA Cup cracker.

1. Sanchez's issues continue

Robert Sanchez lost his place in the Chelsea side for their 4-1 win over Aston Villa in the Premier League on the weekend, with Liam Rosenior bringing in Filip Jorgensen. It felt like a decision that had been coming for a long time, with Sanchez failing to change the perception that he's got a clanger in him.

‌



His goalkeeping for Wrexham's opening goal wasn't of the Clown Academy variety, more not doing the basics well, but the outcome was the same. Smith's first touch from Doyle's long ball was good, yet the way Sanchez back-pedalled, didn't get set and then allowed a weak back-pass of a finish past him into the middle of the goal, without getting a touch on it, was extremely disappointing from a Chelsea perspective. Similarly, the defending of Tosin and Benoit Badiashile left a lot to be desired.

View 6 Images Sam Smith put Wrexham 1-0 up (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

2. Hollywood in the house

It wouldn't be a high-profile televised Wrexham match without the cameras being trained on owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac (as he's now known). This time there was the addition of Reynolds' wife Blake Lively for extra interest.

‌



Reynolds appeared to be on Facetime in the aftermath of Smith's opening goal, with the Hollywood actor waving goodbye to whoever was on the phone as they celebrated. As easy as it is to be cynical, this was exactly the sort of occasion which reminded the viewer just how remarkable Wrexham's story is.

View 6 Images The cameras were trained on the famous actors (Image: James Gill/Getty Images)

3. Garnacho gets lucky

Garnacho is one of those players who gets booed no matter where he is or who he is playing against. And the Wrexham fans were enjoying what they were seeing from the former Manchester United winger in the first half: he was unable to make the most of space on the left wing and sent one attempted volley back to sender at the back post.

‌



But Garnacho had the last laugh ultimately, with his shot ending in an own goal to send Chelsea in level at half-time. It was their only shot on target in the first 45 minutes, despite boasting 71 per cent possession. In truth, they were hardly deserving of their good fortune, but it was to get even better for the pantomime villain winger.

View 6 Images Alejandro Garnacho got lucky with the equaliser (Image: Chelsea FC via Getty Images)

4. Too many changes

Rosenior has explained the need to manage the minutes of his biggest stars and he made nine changes to his line-up for the trip to north Wales, with Cole Palmer and Enzo Fernandez not even travelling. It showed, with Wrexham looking the much more dangerous side for the majority, despite Chelsea dominating possession.

‌



Fringe players did not take their opportunities, with Romeo Lavia passive in central midfield, Badiashile run ragged by Smith and Delap running hard by outshone as soon as Joao Pedro came off the bench. Ultimately it didn't cost them, but the performance was way off their usual standards.

View 6 Images Callum Doyle made it 2-1 to Wrexham (Image: James Gill/Getty Images)

5. Ghost goal

Garnacho's goal was met with a strange reaction. The Argentine cushioned his volley past Okonkwo and wheeled away to celebrate, yet the usual response didn't come - and, for once, that wasn't due to VAR. Garnacho had timed his run perfectly to avoid being offside, but his finish bounced off the stanchion at the back of the goal, flying straight back out, to give some inside the stadium the impression it had hit the post.

‌



It left referee Peter Bankes with the strange task of confirming that, yes, Garnacho had scored. The 21-year-old went to sit on a hoarding in celebration as the home fans gave him a volley of abuse.

6. VAR makes its impact

View 6 Images Lewis Brunt was offside (Image: TNT Sports)

Both of the decisions were the correct ones. Dobson's tackle on Garnacho was reckless and endangered his opponent. And Brunt was offside at the back post from Wrexham's late corner. But the home fans certainly didn't agree, with chants against the technology ringing around the Racecourse Ground.

‌



VAR ended up adding another layer of intrigue to the situation - and ultimately helping Chelsea to get the job done.

Football.title: What Ryan Reynolds did immediately after Wrexham goal vs Chelsea in FA Cup clash
Football.content:
What Ryan Reynolds did immediately after Wrexham goal vs Chelsea in FA Cup clash

Wrexham co-owner Ryan Reynolds was spotted on FaceTime during celebrations as Sam Smith scored against Chelsea, with wife Blake Lively in attendance for the FA Cup fifth round clash

View 3 Images Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney celebrate Wrexham's opening goal against Chelsea (Image: BBC)

Wrexham co-owner Ryan Reynolds was caught on FaceTime during the celebrations of Sam Smith's opening goal against Chelsea in the FA Cup. The Red Dragons hosted the eight-time FA Cup champions in Wales on Saturday evening, hoping to produce another giant-killing act after eliminating Nottingham Forest in the third round.

‌



Chelsea fielded a squad packed with Premier League talent, including Alejandro Garnacho and Pedro Neto, but it was Wrexham who struck first as Smith fired low past Robert Sanchez following Callum Doyle's long pass.

‌



As the players rejoiced with the fans, television cameras panned to Reynolds and Rob McElhenney in their directors' box to capture their reaction. However, Reynolds seemed to be sharing a moment with someone on his mobile.

‌



Reynolds' wife, Blake Lively, accompanied the two Hollywood owners for what represents the club's most significant fixture under their ownership to date.

Smith nearly added a second before the break, whilst Ryan Longman drew a fine stop from Robert Sanchez after 30 minutes.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



The Premier League side equalised just before the interval through a fortunate own goal, though. Garnacho's right-footed effort was cleared off the line by George Thomason, only to deflect off Arthur Okonkwo and cross the line.

Reynolds and McElhenney have been involved with the club since their National League days and have presided over three consecutive promotions, taking them all the way to the Championship.

View 3 Images Ryan Reynolds was spotted on FaceTime (Image: BBC)

‌



The club currently occupy a play-off position after 35 matches, meaning another promotion, to the Premier League, remains within reach.

The co-owners revealed last month that manager Phil Parkinson has a "job for life" at the club after his help in dragging them within touching distance of the top-flight. "I don't know [if] I have the words to fully describe how integral Phil has been to the story and success of Wrexham," McElhenney told The Athletic.

View 3 Images Sam Smith opened the scoring for Wrexham against Chelsea (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"Ryan and I talk all the time, and I just don't see a scenario where Phil Parkinson gets fired. It doesn't make any sense.

"He has been the architect, the creator of this. From our perspective, he's got the job for life. Unless he finds another job he wants to go off and do, he's our coach. He's our manager. He's our guy.

"I know that sounds silly and maybe irresponsible to say publicly, but the truth is that's how we feel. And we have always worn our hearts on our sleeves. Always been as honest as possible."

Football.title: Lewis Hamilton's new partner whose net worth eclipses every F1 driver combined
Football.content:
Lewis Hamilton's new partner whose net worth eclipses every F1 driver combined

Lewis Hamilton's new partner has a stunning net worth that blows away many drivers on the F1 grid

View 6 Images Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton are believed to be an item (Image: /)

Ferrari ace Lewis Hamilton has grabbed headlines with his new love interest, Kim Kardashian. The reality TV queen and the seven-time Formula 1 champion have been spotted together this year.

‌



While Hamilton is known as F1's wealthiest driver, his new flame's net worth actually outshines the combined wealth of the entire F1 line-up.

‌



Calculating the total worth of the current F1 grid requires some guesswork, as while their salaries are public knowledge, their private investments and lifestyle expenses are not. However, based on 2026 financial data and career earnings, the collective net worth of all current F1 drivers is approximately £1.2 billion.

‌



Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Kardashian, 45, who owns a beauty and clothing line among numerous other business ventures, is estimated to be worth around £1.5bn, according to Forbes.

Hamilton is thought to have a net worth of about £380m, making him the richest driver on the circuit. He is followed by Fernando Alonso (£300m) and four-time champion Max Verstappen, whose ever-increasing net worth currently stands at £187m.

‌



Lando Norris calls out 'sneaky' George Russell ahead of 2026 F1 season opener READ MORE :

F1 'monitoring' Middle East situation amid upcoming Bahrain and Saudi Arabia GPs READ MORE :

Kardashian officially entered the billionaire club in 2021. In addition to her Hollywood-based projects, such as the long-standing TV show she stars in with her sisters, her SKIMs empire, which specialises in underwear and clothing, is valued at a whopping £3.7bn alone.

As of 2025, she was thought to have earned over £1.5 million per sponsored Instagram post, according to Backstage. Kardashian and Hamilton have been friends for years.

6 View 6 Images Kim Kardashian is a billionaire (Image: Instagram) 6 View 6 Images Kim Kardashian has attended the 2026 Super Bowl with Lewis Hamilton (Image: PA Archive/PA Images)

‌



They were photographed with their then-partners, Kanye West and Nicole Scherzinger, at the GQ Men of the Year Awards in London in 2014.

However, they have been romantically linked recently, with speculation surfacing after Ferrari's first testing sessions of the 2026 season in Barcelona. The pair were then seen together at the Super Bowl in February.

It was also reported that Hamilton was accompanied by Kim for an intimate couples' retreat in Oxfordshire earlier this month. Sky Sports reporter Ted Kravitz even attempted to extract information from Hamilton recently.

‌



View 6 Images Lewis Hamilton is F1's richest driver (Image: Kym Illman, Getty Images)

He told viewers: "I can tell you he was asked that in the written press. He was asked, 'Did you enjoy your company more than the game at the Super Bowl?'. And he said: 'It's my private life. I'm not talking about that'. So, that's me told."

Yet confirmation doesn't seem to be necessary after the duo have apparently taken their relationship to the next level, having been spotted enjoying a luxurious trip together in Utah. The private location overlook is near Amangiri, an ultra-exclusive five-star desert resort where suites can cost up to £5,000 per night.

‌



Kardashian and Hamilton were also sighted at Estelle Manor in the English Cotswolds. The TV star is thought to have jetted into Oxford Airport on her private plane, estimated to be worth £95m, for a fleeting visit.

6 View 6 Images Kim Kardashian is rumoured to be dating Lewis Hamilton (Image: AFP via Getty Images) 6 View 6 Images Kim Kardashian attends The 2022 Met Gala Celebrating "In America: An Anthology of Fashion" (Image: Getty Images for The Met Museum/)

‌



Kardashian was wedded to rap legend West from 2014 to 2022, with whom she has four children: North, 12, Saint, 9, Chicago, 7, and Psalm, 6.

She was also previously married to ex-basketball pro Kris Humphries from 2011 to 2013, and to music producer Damon Thomas from 2000 to 2004.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Article continues below

Hamilton had a tumultuous relationship with Pussycat Dolls singer Scherzinger from 2007 to 2015. Last year he was rumoured to be dating model Ashley Moore and British vocalist Raye.

Football.title: KSI moment next to Andy Carroll in Dagenham stands says everything about new owner
Football.content:
KSI moment next to Andy Carroll in Dagenham stands says everything about new owner

YouTube star KSI watched on as his new side Dagenham & Redbridge managed to defeat National League South league leaders Dorking Wanderers

View 2 Images New Dagenham & Redbridge minority owner KSI alongside Andy Carroll during their National League South clash with Dorking Wanderers.

New Dagenham & Redbridge minority owner KSI celebrated wildly alongside former England international Andy Carroll after the Daggers took the lead against National League South league leaders Dorking Wanderers. The YouTuber bought a minority share in the club earlier this week and watched on from the stands at Chigwell Construction Stadium on Saturday.

‌



2281 fans welcomed in the new era, with 321 away fans making the trip from Dorking to watch the game. It was the Daggers' third biggest attendance of the season with an additional 1,000 supporters added on to the attendance from the previous home game against AFC Totton.

‌



KSI was greeted by fans as he made his way into the stadium and meeting the players in the dressing room before taking his seat in the stands alongside former Newcastle, Liverpool and West Hame United striker Carroll.

‌



KSI makes immediate decision after buying football club as he follows Ryan Reynolds' lead READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

The 37-year-old, who signed for Dagenham at the start of the season is currently sidelined after ungergoing surgery on a hamstring injury.

It was a tight affair in the first-half, but the Daggers scored the only goal of the game through Aramide Oteh on the hour mark, with KSI embracing Carroll as he celebrated wildly.

‌



The hosts would see out the game and kick start KSI's new ownership regime with a vital three points as they look to move up the National League South table.

The YouTuber took to social media to celebrate the result, and posted on X: "So proud of the boys. What a result! UP THE DAGGERS!!!!!"

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



It was a positive start to life for KSI but there's an awful long way to go before he's fulfilling his dream of taking Dagenham & Redbridge to the Premier League.

Speaking to the club's website after buying minority shares in the club, KSI said: "I'm so excited to start this journey. It's gonna be a rollercoaster for sure, but I hope to bring Dagenham & Redbridge back to the glory days.

"And once we reach that point, I want to go even further. Reaching the Premier League would be a dream. And I believe it is 100% doable.

‌



View 2 Images KSI pictured at Dagenham & Redbridge FC's stadium (Image: After Party Studios)

"It will take a long time, so to the Daggers fans, please be patient. I want you to know that I am fully committed to making sure Dagenham & Redbridge is a team that everyone will know worldwide, and will be an exciting team to watch and support."

KSI will star in a new series charting "every high and low" of football club ownership, with the series to debut on his YouTube channel this summer, reaching his 17million subscribers worldwide and promoted across his socials. It will then continue into the 26/27 football season.

Football.title: Why David Beckham didn't show for controversial Donald Trump White House visit with Inter Miami
Football.content:
Why David Beckham didn't show for controversial Donald Trump White House visit with Inter Miami

Lionel Messi and his Inter Miami team-mates met United States president Donald Trump at the White House to commemorate the club's MLS Cup triumph

View Image Sir David Beckham is a co-owner of Inter Miami CF (Image: (Rich Storry/Getty Images))

Sir David Beckham missed Inter Miami’s White House reception on Thursday due to a 'longstanding commitment'. Lionel Messi and his Inter Miami team-mates met United States president Donald Trump at the White House to commemorate the club's MLS Cup triumph.

‌



However, the event has not been without controversy, as Inter Miami were forced to watch on as Trump addressed the media. During his address, not only did President Trump celebrate Miami’s achievement, but he also provided an update on the ongoing US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

‌



But a notable absence was Beckham, who is a co-owner of the MLS club. However, according to The Times, the former England captain missed the reception at the White House because he had a long-standing commitment with his wife Victoria in Europe.

‌



Amid the fallout and backlash from the reception, Miami’s head coach Javier Mascherano has claimed that he and his squad were told they would 'only talk about football' before their White House meeting.

"I thought we were going to talk football," he said. "We complied with a protocol that is a tradition to come to the White House for being the championship team.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



“It was planned a long time ago for the week we would play here in Washington. We were there a few hours, got to see a little bit of the White House, not much, what we could.

"The contact with President Trump was what was seen on TV, not much more than that. The White House visit was planned between a month and a half and two months ago."

During the reception, President Trump said: "My son [Barron] said, 'Dad, do you know who's going to be there today?' I said, 'no, I've got a lot of things going on'.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

"He said, 'Messi!' He's a big fan of yours. He thinks you're a great person and I think you got to meet a little while ago."

Before he added: "Leo claimed the 47th trophy of his incredible career - the most of all time.

‌



"You could have gone anywhere in the world, any team in the world, and you chose Miami. I just want to thank you for bringing us all on this ride.

"I shouldn't say this because I'm old, but I watched Pele play. I don't know, you may be better than Pele. Pele was pretty good."

Football.title: Adam Wharton's stance on Man Utd transfer and 'defining factor' for deal to happen
Football.content:
Adam Wharton's stance on Man Utd transfer and 'defining factor' for deal to happen

As Adam Wharton continues to impress for Crystal Palace, one important factor could well influence his next move if the England international and Manchester United target is to leave this summer

View 2 Images Adam Wharton looks set to be a man in demand this summer (Image: YouTube/Sky Sports Premier League)

Manchester United target Adam Wharton will reportedly prioritise playing in the Champions League if he decides to leave Crystal Palace in the summer. Wharton, 22, has been a constant positive in an otherwise tumultuous campaign at Selhurst Park.

‌



The England international is expected to have a long line of interested suitors when the transfer window opens this summer. Real Madrid have previously been linked with an audacious move for the ex-Blackburn Rovers midfielder.

‌



However, both Liverpool and United are also understood to be interested in the classy 22-year-old, whose contract in south London expires in July 2029. While both clubs, as well as Manchester City, are still believed to be interested in him, it's understood Wharton, should he opt to move to a new club, will only want a team in the Champions League.

‌



Juan Mata sends Bruno Fernandes message after Man Utd star shows true colours READ MORE :

According to The Sun, the illustrious competition will be the 'defining factor' should he decide to embark on a new challenge. If the Red Devils fail to qualify for Europe's elite competition, they will likely miss out on him entirely.

Michael Carrick's side looked like a firm favourite to finish in the top four after they picked up six victories in the ex-midfielder's first seven Premier League matches in charge.

However, the Red Devils' recent 2-1 away defeat to Newcastle United has made the race for the top four even more tight.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

United are currently in third position, but with only three points separating them and sixth placed Liverpool, with Aston Villa (4th) and Chelsea (5th) also within range, things are perilous for United. With Casemiro leaving the club at the end of the season, the football world knows United will be in the market for a central midfielder.

They have also been linked with Brighton and Hove Albion's Carlos Baleba and Wharton's England team-mate Elliot Anderson of Nottingham Forest.

‌



City have been linked with both midfielders, which is bad news for United, with Pep Guardiola's side almost certainly qualifying for the Champions League.

View 2 Images Adam Wharton could be featuring alongside Kobbie Mainoo at Manchester United next season (Image: 2026 CameraSport)

However, United do have one advantage over their top four rivals. After failing to qualify for Europe last season and also exiting the FA Cup before Carrick's arrival, United do have a long rest between their next Premier League match. Carrick's side do not have a match until their meeting with Aston Villa on March 15 at Old Trafford.

‌



Three days before the clash, Unai Emery's side travel to France to face Lille in the first-leg of their Europa League round of 16 tie. After the defeat to Newcastle, Carrick urged his players and the fan base to remain calm.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Carrick said: "We've lost a game of football and, listen, it hurts me a lot. I'm really, really disappointed with tonight, for different reasons. But we can't lose sight of the position we've put ourselves in as well.

‌



"So, as much as I'm disappointed we need to learn from it, because it's a bit of a lesson for us tonight, in some ways. We can't lose sight of the bigger picture as well. We've got to be better for this."

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp's agent breaks silence as ex-Liverpool boss tipped for return
Football.content:
Jurgen Klopp's agent breaks silence as ex-Liverpool boss tipped for return

Jurgen Klopp is working as Red Bull's global head of football but continues to be linked with a return to club management at Liverpool, Real Madrid or Atletico Madrid

View 2 Images Jurgen Klopp continues to be linked with management jobs (Image: RONNY HARTMANN, AFP via Getty Images)

Jurgen Klopp's agent has insisted that the former Liverpool manager is "very happy" with his current role at Red Bull and links with Real Madrid are "just rumours for now". Klopp is working as Red Bull's global head of football, having left Liverpool in 2024, but continues to be connected with top jobs.

‌



The German emerged as a potential candidate for Real Madrid when they sacked Xabi Alonso in January. Real replaced Alonso with his former team-mate Alvaro Arbeloa, but that hasn't put a stop to the speculation.

‌



Meanwhile, there has also been unfounded speculation that Klopp could return to Liverpool to replace Arne Slot amid a difficult campaign on Merseyside. And reports from Spain have even stated that Atletico Madrid could replace Diego Simeone, who is in the final year of his contract, with Klopp.

‌



The speculation is such that Klopp's representative, Marc Kosicke, has felt the need to go public and deny them. "Jurgen Klopp is very happy with his current role at Red Bull, and what is being said about negotiations to coach Real Madrid are just rumours for now," he said in a statement to 365Scores, relayed by Abola.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Kosicke also specifically denied reports that Klopp could go into international management ahead of the World Cup. "There's no need to answer questions about things that are just rumours," Kosicke added. "Nobody has contacted us at this time."

‌



Klopp has spoken about how much he is enjoying his more relaxed role with Red Bull, where he oversees their multi-club structure with RB Leipzig, New York Red Bulls and Paris FC.

“I’m in a place, as a person, where I’m completely at peace with where I am. I don’t want to be somewhere else,” Klopp told AFP in January. “I don’t get up and excited if Real Madrid are showing interest. If they would be, but it’s the media.

View 2 Images Jurgen Klopp says he is enjoying his current job (Image: John Powell, Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

‌



“Do I want to coach again? At the moment, I would say no, but I cannot say never, never, never. I don’t expect to change my mind, but I don’t know.”

Speaking to The Athletic in September, Klopp hinted that he could use his time with Red Bull as a stepping stone back into management. “I know I can coach a football team, but that doesn't mean I have to do it until my last day,” he said.

“I wanted to do something different. Red Bull gave me an opportunity to find a role which we have been defining together, step by step. I'm in a place as a person where I'm completely at peace with where I am. I don't want to be somewhere else.

‌



“I've got to know a lot of people who I didn't know before. I've been to a lot of business meetings and learned words that I never knew before. It's been a good time. One year in, five years of experience gained.”
